core.label: PHP Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, types, and basic program structure.

topics.label: Installation & Setup
topics.description: Install PHP, configure php.ini, and use the built-in development server. Understand PHP's request lifecycle.

topics.label: Variables & Data Types
topics.description: Strings, integers, floats, booleans, arrays, null. PHP is dynamically typed with loose comparisons.

topics.label: Operators & Type Juggling
topics.description: Arithmetic, comparison (=== vs ==), logical, and null coalescing (??). Strict comparison avoids type juggling bugs.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/elseif/else, switch, match (PHP 8), for, foreach, while. match is a strict-comparison switch.

topics.label: Strings
topics.description: Single/double quotes, heredoc, nowdoc, string functions, and interpolation. Strings are the most manipulated type.

topics.label: Arrays
topics.description: Indexed and associative arrays, array functions (map, filter, reduce, sort). PHP arrays are ordered maps.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: Define functions with type declarations, default parameters, and return types. PHP 8 adds union types and named arguments.

topics.label: Superglobals
topics.description: $_GET, $_POST, $_SESSION, $_COOKIE, $_SERVER, $_ENV. Superglobals are how PHP accesses request data.

topics.label: Include & Require
topics.description: Include PHP files with require, require_once, include. File inclusion is PHP's basic code organization.

topics.label: Error Handling
topics.description: Try/catch exceptions, error levels, set_error_handler. PHP 8 promotes errors to exceptions.

core.label: Object-Oriented PHP
core.description: Classes, interfaces, traits, and OOP patterns.

topics.label: Classes & Objects
topics.description: Define classes with properties, methods, and constructors. OOP is the standard for modern PHP.

topics.label: Constructor Promotion
topics.description: PHP 8 constructor promotion reduces boilerplate for property initialization.

topics.label: Inheritance & abstract
topics.description: Extend classes, override methods, and define abstract classes. PHP supports single inheritance.

topics.label: Interfaces
topics.description: Define contracts with interface. A class can implement multiple interfaces.

topics.label: Traits
topics.description: Reuse methods across classes with traits. Traits solve the single-inheritance limitation.

topics.label: Enums (PHP 8.1)
topics.description: Type-safe enumerations with backed values. Enums replace class constants for finite sets.

topics.label: Readonly Properties & Classes
topics.description: Immutable properties with readonly (PHP 8.1) and readonly classes (PHP 8.2).

topics.label: Magic Methods
topics.description: __construct, __toString, __get, __set, __call, __invoke. Magic methods customize object behavior.

topics.label: Namespaces
topics.description: Organize code with namespaces and use statements. Namespaces prevent class name collisions.

topics.label: Type Declarations
topics.description: Parameter types, return types, union types, intersection types, and nullable (?Type). Strict typing with declare(strict_types=1).

core.label: Composer & Autoloading
core.description: Dependency management, autoloading, and the PHP ecosystem.

topics.label: Composer Basics
topics.description: Install Composer, manage dependencies with composer.json, and understand Packagist. Composer is PHP's package manager.

topics.label: PSR-4 Autoloading
topics.description: Map namespaces to directories with PSR-4. Autoloading eliminates manual require/include.

topics.label: PSR Standards
topics.description: PHP-FIG standards for coding style (PSR-12), logging (PSR-3), HTTP (PSR-7/15). PSRs ensure interoperability.

topics.label: File I/O
topics.description: Read and write files with file_get_contents, file_put_contents, fopen, and SplFileObject.

topics.label: Working with JSON
topics.description: json_encode and json_decode for serialization. JSON is the standard for API communication.

topics.label: Regular Expressions
topics.description: PCRE pattern matching with preg_match, preg_replace, preg_split. Regex is powerful for validation and parsing.

topics.label: Date & Time
topics.description: DateTime, DateTimeImmutable, DateInterval, and Carbon. Immutable DateTime is preferred for safety.

topics.label: Closures & Arrow Functions
topics.description: Anonymous functions with function() and short closures with fn(). Closures capture variables with use().

core.label: Database & PDO
core.description: Database access, prepared statements, and ORM basics.

topics.label: PDO Basics
topics.description: Connect to databases with PDO. PDO is the standard database abstraction layer in PHP.

topics.label: Prepared Statements
topics.description: Prevent SQL injection with parameterized queries. Never concatenate user input into SQL.

topics.label: CRUD Operations
topics.description: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE with PDO. The fundamental database operations.

topics.label: Transactions
topics.description: Atomic operations with beginTransaction, commit, rollBack. Transactions ensure data consistency.

topics.label: Migrations
topics.description: Version-control database schema changes. Migrations are standard in modern PHP frameworks.

topics.label: Query Builder Concepts
topics.description: Build SQL programmatically with a fluent API. Query builders are safer and more readable than raw SQL.

topics.label: Caching Basics
topics.description: Cache database queries and computed results with Redis or Memcached. Caching improves performance dramatically.

topics.label: Database Security
topics.description: SQL injection prevention, password hashing with password_hash/verify, and input sanitization.

core.label: Web Fundamentals
core.description: HTTP, sessions, cookies, and building web applications.

topics.label: HTTP Request/Response
topics.description: Understand the request lifecycle, headers, status codes, and the request-response model.

topics.label: Forms & Input Handling
topics.description: Process GET/POST data, validate input, and handle file uploads. Always validate and sanitize user input.

topics.label: Sessions
topics.description: Maintain state across requests with $_SESSION. Sessions store server-side user data.

topics.label: Cookies
topics.description: Set and read cookies with setcookie(). Cookies store small amounts of client-side data.

topics.label: Security (XSS, CSRF)
topics.description: Prevent cross-site scripting with htmlspecialchars and CSRF with tokens. Security is not optional.

topics.label: HTTP Client (Guzzle)
topics.description: Make HTTP requests with Guzzle. The standard PHP HTTP client library.

topics.label: Templating
topics.description: Separate logic from presentation with Blade, Twig, or plain PHP templates.

topics.label: REST API Basics
topics.description: Build RESTful APIs with proper HTTP methods, status codes, and JSON responses.

core.label: Testing & Code Quality
core.description: PHPUnit, static analysis, and code quality tools.

topics.label: PHPUnit
topics.description: Write unit tests with assertions, data providers, and mocks. PHPUnit is the standard PHP testing framework.

topics.label: Pest
topics.description: Expressive testing with Pest's fluent syntax. Pest wraps PHPUnit with a cleaner API.

topics.label: Mocking
topics.description: Mock dependencies with PHPUnit mocks or Mockery. Isolate units under test.

topics.label: Static Analysis (PHPStan)
topics.description: Find bugs without running code with PHPStan or Psalm. Static analysis catches type errors early.

topics.label: Code Style (PHP CS Fixer)
topics.description: Auto-format code to follow PSR-12 or custom rules. Consistent formatting across the project.

topics.label: Debugging (Xdebug)
topics.description: Step-through debugging, profiling, and coverage with Xdebug. Essential for complex debugging.

topics.label: Logging (Monolog)
topics.description: Structured logging with PSR-3 and Monolog. Proper logging is critical for production debugging.

topics.label: CI/CD for PHP
topics.description: Run tests, linting, and static analysis in GitHub Actions or GitLab CI.
